steps.action: Assyrian folk dances are sets of dances that are with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, partner opposite.
steps.text: Assyrian folk dances are sets of dances that are with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, partner opposite.

steps.action: Assyrian folk dances are mainly made up of circl
steps.text: Assyrian folk dances are mainly made up of circle dances like ballet that are performed in a line, which may be straight, curved, or both.

steps.action: Most of the dances allow unlimited number of par
steps.text: Most of the dances allow unlimited number of participants, with the exception of the Sabre Dance, which require three at most.

steps.action: Assyrian dances would vary from weak to strong,
steps.text: Assyrian dances would vary from weak to strong, depending on the mood and tempo of a song.

steps.action: Assyrian folk dances belong to five metric group
steps.text: Assyrian folk dances belong to five metric groups: 24 (10 dances), 44 (6 dances), 68 (13 dances), 98 (1 dance), 108 (1 dance).

steps.action: The tempo would usually range from slow (70 beat
steps.text: The tempo would usually range from slow (70 beats per minute) to very fast (140 beats).

steps.action: The group performed at several different events
steps.text: The group performed at several different events in Iran and Europe, including the coronation of Mohammad Reza Pahlavi, until its disbandment in 1980.

steps.action: In the years leading up to her leaving Iran, she
steps.text: In the years leading up to her leaving Iran, she allowed American folk dance researcher Rickey Holden to publish booklets and phonographic records detailing Assyrian folk dances.

steps.action: Most Assyrian circle dances are lateral, vining
steps.text: Most Assyrian circle dances are lateral, vining and open-ended, where more and more participants can join the dance.

steps.action: There are only five ways of moving the body; Ste
steps.text: There are only five ways of moving the body; Step, leap, run, hop and jump.

steps.action: The legs are also used to stamp, stomp and kick.
steps.text: The legs are also used to stamp, stomp and kick.

steps.action: In many dances, the torso, along with the should
steps.text: In many dances, the torso, along with the shoulders and arms, bounce up and down rhythmically.

steps.action: Stomps and stamps are also executed in Assyrian
steps.text: Stomps and stamps are also executed in Assyrian folk dances.

steps.action: Knee bends, deep squats and leg extensions are a
steps.text: Knee bends, deep squats and leg extensions are a regular occurrence in Assyrian dance.

steps.action: The "W" arm hold is the most common where the ar
steps.text: The "W" arm hold is the most common where the arms are raised into the "W" position (or, at least, it appears to look like the letter W).

steps.action: The "T" dance is where hands are placed on other
steps.text: The "T" dance is where hands are placed on other participants's shoulders.

steps.action: This is the least common dance of the three.
steps.text: This is the least common dance of the three.

steps.action: It is usually classified as a form of khigga, bu
steps.text: It is usually classified as a form of khigga, but due to its fast performance and style, it is also considered its own dance.

steps.action: Sometimes the khigga beat is geared up to this t
steps.text: Sometimes the khigga beat is geared up to this to indicate a climactic end.

steps.action: The pulsating tempo is consanguineous to the ele
steps.text: The pulsating tempo is consanguineous to the electronic dance music and dance-pop found in western music.

steps.action: The dance technique is virtually like the khigga
steps.text: The dance technique is virtually like the khigga one, albeit it is in a faster fashion and with more emphasis placed on the feet.

steps.action: Alternatively, a simpler form involves the dance
steps.text: Alternatively, a simpler form involves the dancers plainly going forwards and backwards to the beat with stomps, as if they are rocking or swinging.

steps.action: The dance goes by the time signature of 44 and t
steps.text: The dance goes by the time signature of 44 and the tempo is around 120-135bpm.

steps.action: The music accompanying the dance is typically in
steps.text: The music accompanying the dance is typically in the major key.
